source_refs.source_quote: 1 x 2 A slow-acting
IP x4 D

possible_causes: faulty plug connection on the electronics

source_refs.source_quote: F.0 Interruption feed temperature sensor (NTC): NTC faulty, NTC cable faulty, faulty plug connection on NTC, faulty plug connection on the electronics

possible_causes: faulty plug connection on the electronics

source_refs.source_quote: F.1 Interruption return temperature sensor (NTC): NTC faulty, NTC cable faulty, faulty plug connection on NTC, faulty plug connection on the electronics

possible_causes: Sensor plug has mass short to the casing

source_refs.source_quote: F.10 Short-circuit in feed temperature sensor Sensor plug has mass short to the casing, short-circuit in wiring loom, sensor faulty

possible_causes: Sensor plug has mass short to the casing

source_refs.source_quote: F.11 Short-circuit in return temperature sensor Sensor plug has mass short to the casing, short-circuit in wiring loom, sensor faulty

fault_codes.description: Short-circuit on heat retention storage tank sensor
possible_causes: Sensor plug has mass short to the casing

source_refs.source_quote: F.13 Short-circuit on heat retention storage tank sensor Sensor plug has mass short to the casing, short-circuit in wiring loom, sensor faulty

possible_causes: Flow probe not connected thermally correct or defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.20 Safety temperature limiter actuated Flow probe not connected thermally correct or defective, appliance does not shut down

possible_causes: Too little water in the appliance
cable to pump or water shortage switch defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.22 Dry fire Too little water in the appliance, water shortage switch defective, cable to pump or water shortage switch defective, pump blocked or defective, pump output too low

fault_codes.description: Water shortage, temperature spread between flow and return probe too large
possible_causes: flow and return sensor swapped over

source_refs.source_quote: F.23 Water shortage, temperature spread between flow and return probe too large Pump blocked or defective, pump output too low, flow and return sensor swapped over

fault_codes.description: Water shortage, temperature rise too quick

source_refs.source_quote: F.24 Water shortage, temperature rise too quick Pump blocked, low output from the pump, air in appliance, system pressure too low
F.25 Interruption in cable harness Cable harness faulty
F.27 External light: Gas solenoid valve defective, flame detector defective

fault_codes.description: Appliance does not start: Attempts to ignite during start failed
possible_causes: Faults in the gas supply such as: Gas meter or gas pressure detector defective, Air in gas, Gas flow pressure too low, Main gas cock closed
igniter (ignition transformer, ignition cable, ignition plug) defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.28 Appliance does not start: Attempts to ignite during start failed Faults in the gas supply such as: Gas meter or gas pressure detector defective Air in gas Gas flow pressure too low Main gas cock closed Faults in the gas fitting, wrong gas setting, igniter (ignition transformer, ignition cable, ignition plug) defective, ionisation current stopped (cable, electrode), faulty earthing in appliance, electronics defective

fault_codes.description: Flame goes off during operation and subsequent ignition attempts failed

source_refs.source_quote: F.29 Flame goes off during operation and subsequent ignition attempts failed Gas supply temporarily stopped, ignition transformer has spark failure, faulty earthing of appliance

possible_causes: plug not inserted correctly on fan

source_refs.source_quote: F.32 Speed deviation Fan Fans blocked, plug not inserted correctly on fan, hall sensor defective, fault in cable harness, electronics defective

possible_causes: eBUS overload or two power supplies with different polarities on the eBUS

source_refs.source_quote: F.49 eBUS undervoltage Short-circuit on eBUS input, eBUS overload or two power supplies with different polarities on the eBUS

possible_causes: Short-circuit to mass in cable harness to the gas valves
installation fault on gas valve (short-circuit, mass short in the coils)

source_refs.source_quote: F.61 Gas valve control faulty Short-circuit to mass in cable harness to the gas valves, installation fault on gas valve (short-circuit, mass short in the coils) electronics faulty
F.62 Gas valve shutoff delay defective Gas fitting leaking, electronics defective
F.63 EEPROM faulty Electronics defective

possible_causes: Short-circuit in flow or return sensor or electronics defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.64 Electronics/sensor fault Short-circuit in flow or return sensor or electronics defective

possible_causes: Electronics too hot due to external effect

source_refs.source_quote: F.65 Electronics temperature too high Electronics too hot due to external effect, electronics defective

fault_codes.description: Flame monitor input signal is outside the limits (0 or 5 V)

source_refs.source_quote: F.67 Flame monitor input signal is outside the limits (0 or 5 V) electronics defective

fault_codes.description: No valid appliance variant for display and/or electronics
possible_causes: Spare part case: Display and electronics changed at the same time appliance variant not re-set

source_refs.source_quote: F.70 No valid appliance variant for display and/or electronics Spare part case: Display and electronics changed at the same time appliance variant not re-set
F.71 Constant value feed NTC Feed NTC faulty

possible_causes: Flow and/or return sensor is faulty (tolerance too great)

source_refs.source_quote: F.72 Flow and/or return sensor fault Flow and/or return sensor is faulty (tolerance too great)

fault_codes.description: Signal water pressure sensor in the wrong range (too low)
possible_causes: Line to water pressure sensor is interrupted or has a short-circuit to 0V

source_refs.source_quote: F.73 Signal water pressure sensor in the wrong range (too low) Line to water pressure sensor is interrupted or has a short-circuit to 0V or water pressure sensor faulty

fault_codes.description: Signal water pressure sensor in the wrong range (too high)
possible_causes: Line to water pressure sensor has a short-circuit at 5V/24V
internal fault in water pressure sensor

source_refs.source_quote: F.74 Signal water pressure sensor in the wrong range (too high) Line to water pressure sensor has a short-circuit at 5V/24V or internal fault in water pressure sensor

fault_codes.description: No sudden change in pressure was detected on turning on the pump
possible_causes: Water pressure sensor or/and pump defective
Too little water in the appliance

source_refs.source_quote: F.75 No sudden change in pressure was detected on turning on the pump Water pressure sensor or/and pump defective Too little water in the appliance; check adjustable bypass; Connect expansion vessel in return; Air in the appliance
F.76 Interruption in cable harness Cable harness faulty

fault_codes.description: Condensate pump or return signal from accessory module heating
possible_causes: Condensate pump faulty or return signal from the exhaust gas flap has responded

source_refs.source_quote: F.77 Condensate pump or return signal from accessory module heating Condensate pump faulty or return signal from the exhaust gas flap has responded

fault_codes.description: No communication with the printed circuit board
possible_causes: Communication fault between the display and the printed circuit board in the switchgear box

source_refs.source_quote: con No communication with the printed circuit board Communication fault between the display and the printed circuit board in the switchgear box

safety_warnings.text: Immediate danger to life and limb!

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Immediate danger to life and limb!

safety_warnings.text: Risk of death from electric shock!

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of death from electric shock!

safety_warnings.text: Risk of burns or scalding!

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of burns or scalding!

safety_warnings.text: Potentially dangerous situation for the product and environment!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Potentially dangerous situation for the product and environment!

safety_warnings.text: To tighten or loosen screws, only use suitable open-ended spanners (do not use pliers or extensions etc.). Improper use or unsuitable tools can cause damage, such as gas or water leaks.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! To tighten or loosen screws, only use suitable open-ended spanners (do not use pliers or extensions etc.). Improper use or unsuitable tools can cause damage, such as gas or water leaks.

safety_warnings.text: If you smell gas, the following safety instructions must be observed: Do not actuate any electrical switches in the danger area Do not smoke in the danger area Do not use a telephone in the danger area Close the gas stop cock Ventilate the danger area Notify your gas supplier or a suitably qualified heating engineer. National phone number for gas emergencies: 0800 111 999

source_refs.source_quote: If you smell gas, the following safety instructions must be observed: Do not actuate any electrical switches in the danger area Do not smoke in the danger area Do not use a telephone in the danger area • Close the gas stop cock • Ventilate the danger area • Notify your gas supplier or a suitably qualified heating engineer. National phone number for gas emergencies: 0800 111 999

safety_warnings.text: Only use propane in accordance with DIN 51622 or EN 437.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Only use propane in accordance with DIN 51622 or EN 437.

safety_warnings.text: Installation and maintenance of the unit may only be undertaken by a competent person approved at the time by the Health and Safety Executive in accordance with the "Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulations 1998". In IE the installation must comply with the current Version of I.S.813 'Domestic Gas Installations' and the current Building Regulations. The current ETCI Regulations for the installation of electrical equipment must also be observed.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Installation and maintenance of the unit may only be undertaken by a competent person approved at the time by the Health and Safety Executive in accordance with the "Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulations 1998". In IE the installation must comply with the current Version of I.S.813 'Domestic Gas Installations' and the current Building Regulations. The current ETCI Regulations for the installation of electrical equipment must also be observed.

safety_warnings.text: With regards to the Manual Handling Operations, 1992 Regulations, the following lift operation exceeds the recommended weight for a one man lift.

source_refs.source_quote: Important: With regards to the Manual Handling Operations, 1992 Regulations, the following lift operation exceeds the recommended weight for a one man lift.

safety_warnings.text: Danger of death by electric shock! If a room sealed boiler is installed in a room with a bath or a shower, the electrical switches and the boiler controller, which operate at mains voltage must be mounted in locations where any person in the bath or in the shower cannot reach them

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Danger of death by electric shock! If a room sealed boiler is installed in a room with a bath or a shower, the electrical switches and the boiler controller, which operate at mains voltage must be mounted in locations where any person in the bath or in the shower cannot reach them

safety_warnings.text: Do not install the appliance in rooms prone to frost. In rooms with aggressive steam or dust, the appliance must be operated independently of the ventilation!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Do not install the appliance in rooms prone to frost. In rooms with aggressive steam or dust, the appliance must be operated independently of the ventilation!

safety_warnings.text: The Vaillant ecoTEC exclusive may only be installed by a suitably qualified heating engineer approved at the time by the Health and Safety Executive who also assumes the responsibility for installing the appliance properly and fully commissioning the appliance prior to first use, along with demonstrating its correct use to the end user.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! The Vaillant ecoTEC exclusive may only be installed by a suitably qualified heating engineer approved at the time by the Health and Safety Executive who also assumes the responsibility for installing the appliance properly and fully commissioning the appliance prior to first use, along with demonstrating its correct use to the end user.

safety_warnings.text: Flush the heating system thoroughly before connecting the appliance! By doing that, residue such as welds, cinder, hemp, putty, rust, rough dust and similar substances are removed from the pipes. Otherwise such substances can be deposited in the appliance and cause damage.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Flush the heating system thoroughly before connecting the appliance! By doing that, residue such as welds, cinder, hemp, putty, rust, rough dust and similar substances are removed from the pipes. Otherwise such substances can be deposited in the appliance and cause damage.

safety_warnings.text: The gas installation may only be established by an authorised engineer approved at the time by the Health and Safety Executive. The legal directives and the local regulations for gas supply companies must be observed.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! The gas installation may only be established by an authorised engineer approved at the time by the Health and Safety Executive. The legal directives and the local regulations for gas supply companies must be observed.

safety_warnings.text: Ensure stress-free assembly of the gas pipes to avoid leakages!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Ensure stress-free assembly of the gas pipes to avoid leakages!

safety_warnings.text: The gas regulating block may be tested for leakage only with a maximum pressure of 110 mbar! The operating pressure may not exceed 60 mbar. If these pressures are exceeded, the gas fitting may be damaged.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! The gas regulating block may be tested for leakage only with a maximum pressure of 110 mbar! The operating pressure may not exceed 60 mbar. If these pressures are exceeded, the gas fitting may be damaged.

safety_warnings.text: Mount the hot water and cold water lines so they are tension-free, this prevents leaks!

source_refs.section_title: 5.3 Hot water and cold water connections
source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Mount the hot water and cold water lines so they are tension-free, this prevents leaks!

safety_warnings.text: Mount the heating lines so they are tension-free, this prevents leaks!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Mount the heating lines so they are tension-free, this prevents leaks!

safety_warnings.text: Vaillant appliances are only system-certified if genuine Vaillant flue pipes are used. Only use genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Malfunctions can occur if you use other accessories. These may result in damage and injury. You will find a list of genuine flue pipes in the Vaillant installation manual for flue pipes. The CE mark is valid only if the appliance is operated with Vaillant flue pipes.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Vaillant appliances are only system-certified if genuine Vaillant flue pipes are used. Only use genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Malfunctions can occur if you use other accessories. These may result in damage and injury. You will find a list of genuine flue pipes in the Vaillant installation manual for flue pipes. The CE mark is valid only if the appliance is operated with Vaillant flue pipes.

safety_warnings.text: The electrical installation may only be undertaken by an authorised engineer. Risk of fatal electric shock from touching live connections. Always disconnect the power supply first by pulling the plug out of the wall socket. Only after this can the installation be undertaken. Continuous voltage is present on the mains connection terminals L and N, even if the main switch is turned off!

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! The electrical installation may only be undertaken by an authorised engineer. Risk of fatal electric shock from touching live connections. Always disconnect the power supply first by pulling the plug out of the wall socket. Only after this can the installation be undertaken. Continuous voltage is present on the mains connection terminals L and N, even if the main switch is turned off!

safety_warnings.text: Do not connect mains voltage to the terminals 7, 8, 9! Danger of destroying the electronics!

source_refs.section_title: 5.10.2 Connection of controllers, accessories and external installation components
source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Do not connect mains voltage to the terminals 7, 8, 9! Danger of destroying the electronics!

safety_warnings.text: When using an flow header we recommend turning off the speed control and setting the pump output to 100%.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! When using an flow header we recommend turning off the speed control and setting the pump output to 100%.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of injury and risk of damage to property due to neglected inspection and maintenance! Neglected inspection and maintenance works or not observing the stated inspection and maintenance intervals can interfere with the operational safety of the boiler and can result in damage to property and to persons. Point out to the operator that he must observe the demanded inspection and maintenance intervals as a minimum. Carry out proper regular inspections once a year. Carry out regular maintenance as dictated by findings during the inspection process. The frequency of maintenance must not be longer than every 5 years.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of injury and risk of damage to property due to neglected inspection and maintenance! Neglected inspection and maintenance works or not observing the stated inspection and maintenance intervals can interfere with the operational safety of the boiler and can result in damage to property and to persons. • Point out to the operator that he must observe the demanded inspection and maintenance intervals as a minimum. • Carry out proper regular inspections once a year. • Carry out regular maintenance as dictated by findings during the inspection process. The frequency of maintenance must not be longer than every 5 years.

safety_warnings.text: Danger of life and limb due to improper inspection/maintenance! Inspections/Maintenance work carried out improperly can result in leakages and explosion. The boiler may only be inspected/maintained by a competent person.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Danger of life and limb due to improper inspection/maintenance! Inspections/Maintenance work carried out improperly can result in leakages and explosion. • The boiler may only be inspected/maintained by a competent person.

safety_warnings.text: Possible poisoning and burning by escaping hot flue gases! It is possible that hot flue gases escape and could result in poisoning or burning, if the boiler is operated without a completely installed air/flue gas duct with an opened air/flue gas duct with internal leakages and an opened front casing. Operate the boiler for commissioning for testing purposes in continuous mode only with closed front casing and completely mounted and closed air/flue gas duct.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Possible poisoning and burning by escaping hot flue gases! It is possible that hot flue gases escape and could result in poisoning or burning, if the boiler is operated without a completely installed air/flue gas duct with an opened air/flue gas duct with internal leakages and an opened front casing. • Operate the boiler for commissioning for testing purposes in continuous mode only with closed front casing and completely mounted and closed air/flue gas duct.

safety_warnings.text: Danger of life and limb by electric shock! The supply terminals of the boiler are under mains voltage even if the boiler main switch is off. Don't touch the supply terminals. Protect the electronic box from any water or spray. Before working on the boiler, turn off the power and secure against restart.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Danger of life and limb by electric shock! The supply terminals of the boiler are under mains voltage even if the boiler main switch is off. • Don't touch the supply terminals. • Protect the electronic box from any water or spray. • Before working on the boiler, turn off the power and secure against restart.

safety_warnings.text: Increased risk of poisoning due to incorrect settings! Incorrect setting may increase the risk of poisoning. If one of the flue gas values is greater than the acceptable values in Table 8.1 then check the integrity of the complete flue gas installation the integrity of the combustion circuit seals the gas inlet working pressure the gas flow rate.

source_refs.section_title: 8.1.4 Adjusting the CO2 concentration (or the air ratio)
source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Increased risk of poisoning due to incorrect settings! Incorrect setting may increase the risk of poisoning. • If one of the flue gas values is greater than the acceptable values in Table 8.1 then check the integrity of the complete flue gas installation the integrity of the combustion circuit seals the gas inlet working pressure the gas flow rate.

safety_warnings.text: Risk to life due to poisoning! CO is an extremely toxic gas. Risk to life due to excessive CO concentrations. If you are not able to adjust the boiler correctly and the flue gas values remain higher than allowed in Table 8.1, call the Vaillant Service Solutions. Do not start up the boiler!

source_refs.section_title: 8.1.4 Adjusting the CO2 concentration (or the air ratio)
source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk to life due to poisoning! CO is an extremely toxic gas. Risk to life due to excessive CO concentrations. • If you are not able to adjust the boiler correctly and the flue gas values remain higher than allowed in Table 8.1, call the Vaillant Service Solutions. • Do not start up the boiler!

safety_warnings.text: Danger of explosion through gas leakage! The mixer tube between the gas control unit and burner must not be opened. It can only be guaranteed that this component is gas-tight after it has been inspected at the factory.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Danger of explosion through gas leakage! The mixer tube between the gas control unit and burner must not be opened. It can only be guaranteed that this component is gas-tight after it has been inspected at the factory.

safety_warnings.topic: Burns or scalding from hot components
safety_warnings.text: There is danger of being burned or scalded at the compact thermal module and at all components carrying water. Only carry out work on these components once they have cooled down.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! There is danger of being burned or scalded at the compact thermal module and at all components carrying water. Only carry out work on these components once they have cooled down.

safety_warnings.text: Damage to the gas pipe! Under no circumstance may the compact thermal module be suspended from the flexible corrugated gas pipe.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Damage to the gas pipe! Under no circumstance may the compact thermal module be suspended from the flexible corrugated gas pipe.

safety_warnings.text: Protect the electronics box turned down against sprayed water.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Protect the electronics box turned down against sprayed water.

safety_warnings.text: The two seals (1 - Fig. 8.5) on the burner assembly (must be replaced each time the module is removed (for example during maintenance). The burner flange insulation (2- Fig. 8.5) on the compact thermal module (SP no. 21-0734) may not show any signs of damage; otherwise it must also be replaced.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! The two seals (1 - Fig. 8.5) on the burner assembly (must be replaced each time the module is removed (for example during maintenance). The burner flange insulation (2- Fig. 8.5) on the compact thermal module (SP no. 21-0734) may not show any signs of damage; otherwise it must also be replaced.

safety_warnings.text: Open the gas supply and check the appliance for gas leaks using a leak testing spray. Pay particular attention to the gas fitting (6).

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Open the gas supply and check the appliance for gas leaks using a leak testing spray. Pay particular attention to the gas fitting (6).

safety_warnings.text: If the device is operated with empty condense trap, there is risk of poisoning through emitting flue gases. Therefore, fill the trap with water again after each cleaning operation.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! If the device is operated with empty condense trap, there is risk of poisoning through emitting flue gases. Therefore, fill the trap with water again after each cleaning operation.

safety_warnings.text: Each time the components are replaced, comply with the safety instructions below for your own safety and to avoid damage to the appliance.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Each time the components are replaced, comply with the safety instructions below for your own safety and to avoid damage to the appliance.

safety_warnings.text: Isolate the appliance from the electrical mains by pulling the plug out of the wall plug socket!

source_refs.source_quote: Note! Isolate the appliance from the electrical mains by pulling the plug out of the wall plug socket!

safety_warnings.text: Before replacing the component, comply with the safety instructions in Section 10.1.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Before replacing the component, comply with the safety instructions in Section 10.1.

safety_warnings.text: Mount the gas valve and the fan in the same position as before.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Mount the gas valve and the fan in the same position as before.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check the air flue gas installation
maintenance_tasks.description: Check for leaks, proper fixation, blockages, damage, and compliance with installation instructions.
maintenance_tasks.interval: annually

source_refs.source_quote: Check the air flue gas installation for leaks and for proper fixation and ensure it is not blocked or damaged and is fitted correctly, complying with the relevant installation instructions.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: General inspection and cleaning of the boiler
maintenance_tasks.description: Carry out a general inspection of the boiler for dirt and dust and clean as necessary.
maintenance_tasks.interval: annually

source_refs.source_quote: Carry out a general inspection of the boiler for dirt and dust and clean as necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Visually inspect the heat engine
maintenance_tasks.description: Visually inspect the complete heat engine for its general condition and for signs of corrosion, sooting or other forms of damage. If damage is evident proceed to column 2.
maintenance_tasks.interval: annually

source_refs.source_quote: Visually inspect the complete heat engine for its general condition and for signs of corrosion, sooting or other forms of damage. If damage is evident proceed to column 2.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Measure the gas flow rate
maintenance_tasks.description: Measure the gas flow rate during operation with maximum load (Section 6.2.2). If the gas flow rate complies to the Table 6.3 continue with column 1, if not proceed to column 2.
maintenance_tasks.interval: annually

source_refs.source_quote: Measure the gas flow rate during operation with maximum load (Section 6.2.2). If the gas flow rate complies to the Table 6.3 continue with column 1, if not proceed to column 2.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check the gas inlet working pressure
maintenance_tasks.description: Check the gas inlet working pressure (Section 6.2.3) operation with maximum load. If the gas inlet working pressure complies to the Table 6.4 continue with column 1, if not proceed to column 2.
maintenance_tasks.interval: annually

source_refs.source_quote: Check the gas inlet working pressure (Section 6.2.3) operation with maximum load. If the gas inlet working pressure complies to the Table 6.4 continue with column 1, if not proceed to column 2.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check combustion by measuring CO, CO2 and CO/CO2
maintenance_tasks.description: If the values are outside the tolerances of Table 8.1 proceed to maintenance column 2. You must not proceed with the maintenance if a new burner door seal kit is not available.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check combustion by measuring CO, CO2 and CO/CO2. If the values are outside the tolerances of Table 8.1 proceed to maintenance column 2. You must not proceed with the maintenance if a new burner door seal kit is not available.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Isolate the boiler from the power mains and check electrical connections
maintenance_tasks.description: Isolate the boiler from the power mains. Check whether the electrical plug connections and the other electrical connections are fitted tightly and correct them if necessary.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Isolate the boiler from the power mains. Check whether the electrical plug connections and the other electrical connections are fitted tightly and correct them if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Close the gas isolation valve and the service valves
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Close the gas isolation valve and the service valves.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Dump the pressure in the boiler on the water side and check the charge pressure of the expansion vessel
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Dump the pressure in the boiler on the water side (observe pressure gauge) and check the charge pressure of the expansion vessel. Top up if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Remove the compact thermal module
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Remove the compact thermal module.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check the integrity of all combustion circuit seals
maintenance_tasks.description: Especially the burner door seal. If there are any damages repair them before proceeding.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check the integrity of all combustion circuit seals, especially the burner door seal. If there are any damages repair them before proceeding.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Clean the heat exchanger
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Clean the heat exchanger.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check and clean the burner
maintenance_tasks.description: Check whether the burner is dirty and clean it if necessary.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check whether the burner is dirty and clean it if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check the condensate siphon in the boiler, clean and fill if necessary
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check the condensate siphon in the boiler, clean and fill if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check the condensate ducts in the boiler and clean if necessary
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check the condensate ducts in the boiler and clean if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Install the compact thermal module
maintenance_tasks.description: Caution: Use new seals and nuts!
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Install the compact thermal module. Caution: Use new seals and nuts!

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Open the service valves and fill up the boiler/appliance
maintenance_tasks.description: Fill to approximately 1.0 - 2.0 bar (depending on the static height of the system). Start the bleeding program P.0.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Open the service valves and fill up the boiler/appliance to approximately 1.0 - 2.0 bar (depending on the static height of the system). Start the bleeding program P.0.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Open the gas isolation valve, reconnect the boiler with the power mains and switch on the boiler
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Open the gas isolation valve, reconnect the boiler with the power mains and switch on the boiler.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Perform a test operation of the boiler and heating installation
maintenance_tasks.description: Including water heating and bleed again if necessary.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Perform a test operation of the boiler and heating installation, including water heating and bleed again if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check visually the ignition and burner performance
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check visually the ignition and burner performance.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check the boiler for leaks of any kind
maintenance_tasks.description: (gas, flue gas, water, condensate) and rectify as necessary.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Check the boiler for leaks of any kind (gas, flue gas, water, condensate) and rectify as necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check CO, CO2, CO/CO2-values again
maintenance_tasks.description: If you had problems with the CO, CO2, CO/CO2-values in Step 6 before the maintenance, check them again now (see Table 8.1). If they are outside of the tolerances of Table 8.1 make an adjustment, see Section 8.1.4.
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: If you had problems with the CO, CO2, CO/CO2-values in Step 6 before the maintenance, check them again now (see Table 8.1). If they are outside of the tolerances of Table 8.1 make an adjustment, see Section 8.1.4.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Complete the gas commission checklist (benchmark book)
maintenance_tasks.interval: at regular intervals, but no longer than 5 years

source_refs.source_quote: Complete the gas commission checklist (benchmark book).
